summary: Implemented M4 campaign planning UI read-model projection, command DTO submission controls, supply/route risk review, siege/withdrawal choices, AI reason display, and war report rendering without storing WorldState or modifying sim-core.

tests_run.result: Working tree contained only allowed task changes under apps/web, packages/client-core, packages/ui, tests, and project task/handoff paths.
1 file passed; 11 tests passed, including M4 campaign read-model projection and command DTO builders.
1 file passed; 5 tests passed, including M4 campaign planning, supply/route risk, AI reason, and war report server render coverage.
1 file passed; 3 tests passed with bootstrapped M4 read-model assertions.
Vite production web build passed; Vite reported existing chunk-size warnings for large bundles.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic sim runner matched; 33 Vitest files passed with 237 tests; web Vitest passed.
Initial run had 4/5 passing; 1440px viewport overflow assertion failed by 1px after M4 layout addition.
5 Playwright tests passed after CSS layout fix, including M4 campaign planning command flow, supply/route forecast, siege/withdrawal command flow, AI reasons, and war report rendering.
Parsed task JSON; id matched; status REVIEW; route_to qa_reviewer; allowed_paths 12; forbidden_paths 22.
Initial post-handoff check stopped at Prettier formatting warnings in packages/client-core/src/index.ts, packages/ui/src/index.tsx, and tests/client-core-m2-district-list.test.ts.
Formatted the three changed files reported by pnpm check.
Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

decisions: Kept M4 client read-models as disposable UI slices in client-core and did not add Redux or store full WorldState.
Used protocol command DTO builders for plan, cancel, start march, siege choice, and withdrawal submission instead of mutating UI state as authority.
Did not change sim-core, protocol command/query schemas, Electron, platform, save-format, or production dependencies.
Used Playwright coverage rather than Storybook because the repository already has e2e web-shell coverage and no apps/storybook directory.
risks: Client-core mirrors protocol-visible M4 query result row shapes as thin projection input interfaces because those result interfaces are currently package-internal to sim-core and not exported from protocol.
The current web shell still uses fixture-backed read-model snapshots; reviewer should verify this remains acceptable for M4 UI coverage and does not imply authoritative state.
requested_action: Perform independent QA review of M4 client campaign planning UI against the task acceptance criteria, path constraints, command/query boundaries, viewport behavior, and test evidence.
